summary.text: Coding agent / SWE agent projects continue to command the highest developer attention on GitHub, with four repos in the top five and an average daily delta of 2,007 stars. Understand-Anything added 4,365 stars in 24 hours, while DeepSeek-Reasonix accelerated to 1,599 stars, both showing sustained momentum. The category's dominance is reinforced by cross-source signals: a Hacker News thread titled 'I think Anthropic and OpenAI have found product-market fit' (805 votes) echoes the theme of agentic coding tools gaining traction. Meanwhile, MCP / Tool Calling projects like ECC (1,628 stars) maintain steady interest, but no new category has emerged to challenge coding agents. The sustained focus suggests developers are actively investing in agentic coding workflows, not just experimenting.

projects.name: Understand-Anything
projects.description: Graphs that teach > graphs that impress. Turn any code into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and more.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Turn any codebase, knowledge base, or docs into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers joining large codebases struggle to understand architecture and business logic. This tool provides an interactive knowledge graph with guided tours, semantic search, and diff impact analysis to accelerate onboarding and code comprehension.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded in popularity with 38,517 stars, a 24h star delta of 6,337, a three-day average of 5,140, and an acceleration of 1.2 (medium). It has a recent release and a commit within the last 7 days, indicating active development and strong community interest.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The trigger is unclear from the provided data, but the sustained high star velocity and recent commits suggest ongoing feature additions or viral adoption among AI coding tool users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI) by providing a visual, searchable knowledge graph that makes large codebases understandable. The multi-platform support and easy installation lower the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This tool can dramatically reduce onboarding time for new team members and help existing developers quickly grasp complex codebases. It also supports knowledge base analysis, making it useful for documentation and wiki exploration.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The trend signals a growing demand for AI-assisted code comprehension tools that go beyond simple chat. Developers want structured, visual representations of codebases that integrate seamlessly with their existing AI workflows. This could become a standard plugin for AI coding agents.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Software developers, engineering managers, technical leads, and anyone working with large or unfamiliar codebases. Also useful for teams using AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, and Gemini CLI.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on LLM-based analysis which may produce inaccurate summaries for complex or poorly documented code. The knowledge graph is stored as JSON and may become large for huge monorepos, requiring git-lfs. The tool is still relatively new and may have undiscovered bugs or limitations.

projects.name: Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
projects.description: 754 structured cybersecurity skills for AI agents · Mapped to 5 frameworks: MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND & NIST AI RMF · agentskills.io standard · Works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex CLI, Cursor, Gemini CLI & 20+ platforms · 26 security domains · Apache 2.0

llmSummary.oneLiner: 754 structured cybersecurity skills for AI agents, mapped to 5 frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND, NIST AI RMF) and compatible with 26+ AI platforms.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack structured domain knowledge to perform cybersecurity tasks like a senior analyst. This library provides step-by-step playbooks that agents can search and execute, bridging the gap between generic LLMs and expert-level security workflows.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 10,623 stars, 1,233 forks, and a 24-hour star delta of 1,176 (three-day average 974.5). Acceleration is 1.2 (medium). It is trending as a sustained hot project, indicating strong and growing interest from the developer and security community.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The trigger is unclear. The last commit was on 2026-05-13, but there is no recent release and no commit within the last 7 days. The sustained star growth may be driven by organic discovery, word-of-mouth, or external mentions rather than a single event.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It fills a clear gap: giving AI agents structured cybersecurity skills. With the cybersecurity workforce shortage and the rise of AI coding agents, developers and security teams are eager for tools that make agents more capable in security tasks. The cross-framework mapping and broad platform compatibility add to its appeal.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a ready-to-use knowledge base for any AI agent that needs cybersecurity capabilities. Developers can clone it and integrate it with Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, and others to instantly enhance their agent's security skills.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift toward domain-specific skill libraries for AI agents. Developers building agentic workflows should consider adopting structured skill standards (like agentskills.io) to make their agents more useful in specialized domains. The progressive disclosure architecture (30 tokens to scan, 500-2000 to load) is a practical pattern for managing large skill sets within context windows.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Security analysts, SOC teams, penetration testers, DevSecOps engineers, AI/ML developers building security agents, and anyone using AI coding assistants for cybersecurity tasks.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is community-created and not affiliated with Anthropic. The README contains promotional links (e.g., survey, playground) that may be self-serving. The quality and accuracy of the skills depend on community contributions; users should validate skills before using in production.

projects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, engineered around prefix-cache stability to keep token costs low across long sessions.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Generic AI coding agents waste tokens on repeated context re-processing. Reasonix locks the prefix cache so that long-running sessions maintain >99% cache hit rates, slashing API costs by ~80% compared to uncached usage.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 3,097 stars in the last 24 hours and 1,922 stars yesterday, with a 7-day average delta of 1,515.5 and an acceleration ratio of 2 (medium). This is a sudden breakout — the project crossed 11,987 stars with 685 forks and 286 open issues, indicating explosive community interest. The velocity badges (Top 2 in Agents, Top 3 in LLMs, Top 3 in CLI) confirm it's one of the fastest-growing repos in its category.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and the detailed README with benchmarks showing 99.82% cache hit and cost savings likely drove a wave of adoption. The addition of a desktop client prerelease and QQ channel integration may have also expanded the user base.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It offers a practical, cost-saving solution for developers who use DeepSeek models extensively. The focus on prefix-cache stability is a unique selling point that directly addresses a pain point (high API costs) without sacrificing functionality. The polished CLI, extensive configuration, and community features (Discord, QQ) lower the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use DeepSeek models for coding tasks, Reasonix can dramatically reduce your token spend while maintaining a full-featured agent loop. The architecture is worth studying for anyone building cache-aware LLM applications.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward model-specific optimizations in AI tooling. Rather than building generic agents that work with any LLM, Reasonix goes all-in on DeepSeek's prefix-cache mechanic, achieving cost efficiencies that generic frameworks cannot match. Developers should consider whether their workflows can benefit from similar cache-aware designs.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers who use DeepSeek models for coding assistance, especially those running long interactive sessions or batch processing. Also relevant for teams looking to reduce API costs without switching away from DeepSeek.
llmSummary.riskNotes: DeepSeek-only — if you switch models, Reasonix loses its core advantage. The project is relatively new (11.9k stars but 286 open issues), so stability and feature completeness may still be evolving. The desktop client is prerelease and not code-signed, which may deter some users.

projects.name: ECC
projects.description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

llmSummary.oneLiner: ECC is a harness-native operator system that optimizes performance, security, and continuous learning for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It solves the fragmentation and inefficiency of configuring AI agent harnesses by providing a unified system of skills, hooks, memory optimization, security scanning, and research-first development workflows.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 195,517 stars, 30,086 forks, and a sustained 1,794.5 three-day average star delta, plus a 1.5x acceleration and a recent release (v2.0.0-rc.1) within 30 days, ECC is experiencing massive, sustained interest. The project has a recent commit within 7 days and is an Anthropic Hackathon winner, making it a top-tier tool for AI agent developers.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The recent v2.0.0-rc.1 release added a dashboard GUI, operator workflows, prediction-market skills, optimization skill packs, an ECC 2.0 alpha Rust control-plane, and ecosystem hardening. This surface refresh and expansion likely drove the latest star surge.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ECC is trending because it provides a comprehensive, production-ready system for AI agent harnesses, addressing a critical need for developers building with Claude Code, Codex, and similar tools. Its massive star count and sustained growth reflect its utility and community adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: ECC is a must-evaluate tool for any developer using AI coding agents. It offers a complete, open-source system to optimize agent performance, security, and learning, with cross-harness support and a vibrant community.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward standardized, performance-optimized agent harnesses. Developers should adopt ECC to reduce configuration overhead, improve agent reliability, and leverage community-driven skills and security practices.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers, Claude Code/Codex/Cursor users, MCP tool builders, and teams building production-grade agentic workflows.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is maintained by a single primary maintainer, which could pose bus-factor risk. The rapid pace of releases may introduce instability. The README's star count (195K) seems unusually high for a niche tool; verify authenticity.

projects.name: nanobot
projects.description: Lightweight, open-source AI agent for your tools, chats, and workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: An ultra-lightweight, open-source AI agent that connects LLMs to tools, chats, and workflows with minimal overhead.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Existing AI agents are often heavy, complex, or tied to specific platforms. nanobot provides a small, readable core loop that supports multiple chat channels, memory, MCP, and practical deployment paths, enabling developers to quickly build and run personal agents.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 43,292 stars, a 24-hour delta of +78, and a 7-day average delta of 53.3, the project shows strong, sustained growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.5 indicates steady momentum without sudden spikes, suggesting organic adoption. Recent releases (v0.2.0 on 2026-05-15) and daily commits keep it fresh and actively developed.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project released v0.2.0 on 2026-05-15, introducing /goal for sustained objectives, a WebUI shipped inside the wheel, image generation, and 5 new providers with fallback models. This feature-rich release likely drove the recent star acceleration.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines the simplicity of OpenClaw with the power of Claude Code and Codex, offering a lightweight yet extensible agent that works with multiple LLM providers and chat platforms. The active development, frequent releases, and comprehensive documentation make it appealing to developers seeking a practical, open-source agent framework.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: nanobot demonstrates that a minimal, readable agent loop can be production-ready. Developers can use it as a base for custom agents, integrate it with their tools via MCP, or deploy it as a personal assistant across Telegram, Discord, Slack, and more.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The trend toward lightweight, multi-provider, multi-channel agents is accelerating. nanobot's approach of keeping the core small while supporting plugins and MCP aligns with the industry shift toward modular, composable AI systems. Developers should watch for similar patterns in agent frameworks.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers building personal AI assistants, teams integrating LLMs into workflows, and anyone wanting a lightweight, open-source alternative to proprietary agent tools.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still relatively new (v0.2.0) and may have breaking changes. With 891 open issues, some stability concerns exist. The rapid release pace could lead to feature bloat or documentation lag.

projects.name: open-design
projects.description: 🎨 Local-first, open-source  Claude Design alternative. ⚡ 19 Skills · ✨ 71 brand-grade Design Systems 🖼 Generate web · desktop · mobile prototypes · slides · images · videos · HyperFrames 📦 Sandboxed preview · HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export 🤖 Runs on Claude Code / Codex / Cursor / Gemini / OpenCode / Qwen / Copilot / Hermes / Kimi CLI.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Open-source, local-first alternative to Claude Design that wires 16 coding-agent CLIs into a skill-driven design workflow with 132 skills and 150 design systems.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Claude Design is closed-source, paid-only, cloud-only, and locked to Anthropic's model. Open Design provides a fully open, local-first, BYOK alternative that works with any coding agent CLI or OpenAI-compatible proxy, enabling artifact-first design without vendor lock-in.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 54,261 stars, a 24-hour star delta of 1,121, and a sustained 7-day average of 820 stars per day, this project is experiencing explosive organic growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.4 indicates steady, high-velocity momentum without sudden spikes, suggesting deep community resonance. The recent 0.8.0-preview release and active iteration on main signal a project that is rapidly maturing and capturing attention from both developers and designers.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project recently released version 0.8.0-preview, which is described as 'the next phase of Open Design.' The README explicitly calls for community contributions to push the project forward, and the commit activity remains high with a recent commit within 7 days. The sustained star growth and high fork count (6,149) indicate ongoing viral spread, likely amplified by social media and developer communities.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It fills a massive gap: a high-quality, open-source alternative to a popular but locked-down AI design tool. The BYOK approach, support for 16 different coding agent CLIs, and local-first architecture appeal to developers who want control and flexibility. The project also benefits from being built on top of other open-source projects (huashu-design, guizang-ppt-skill, open-codesign, multica), creating a collaborative ecosystem.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates that AI-assisted design can be fully open and agent-agnostic. Developers can integrate it into their workflows without being tied to a single vendor, and the modular skill/design-system architecture makes it extensible. It's a practical example of how to build a local-first AI tool that leverages existing CLI agents.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The success of Open Design signals a strong market demand for open, composable AI tools that prioritize user ownership and flexibility. For AI developers, this indicates an opportunity to build similar 'agent orchestration' layers that decouple AI capabilities from specific models or providers. The local-first, BYOK model reduces friction and trust barriers, which could become a standard expectation for developer tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and designers who want an open-source, local-first alternative to Claude Design; teams that need to generate design prototypes, slides, images, or videos using AI without cloud dependency; builders who want to integrate AI design into their own workflows using existing coding agents.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still in preview (0.8.0-preview) and may have stability or compatibility issues. It relies on external CLI agents being installed and configured correctly, which could be a barrier for non-technical users. The rapid growth may strain maintainer capacity, and the project's long-term sustainability depends on community contributions and funding.

projects.name: antigravity-awesome-skills
projects.description: Installable GitHub library of 1,400+ agentic skills for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, and more. Includes installer CLI, bundles, workflows, and official/community skill collections.

llmSummary.oneLiner: An installable library of 1,480+ reusable agentic skills (SKILL.md playbooks) for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and other AI coding assistants, with an npm installer and tool-specific setup.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Eliminates the need to collect and manage one-off prompt snippets by providing a searchable, installable catalog of structured skills that give AI coding assistants better context, constraints, and outputs for recurring tasks.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 38,951 stars, a 24-hour delta of +235, and an acceleration ratio of 2.1 (medium, above the 2.0 threshold for sudden breakout), this repo is experiencing a clear velocity spike. The three-day average star delta of 113.5 and the rule-based trend type of 'sudden_breakout' confirm a rapid increase in attention momentum, likely driven by the recent V11.8.0 release and growing adoption of AI coding assistants.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The latest release V11.8.0 (within 30 days) expanded the skill count to 1,480+ and updated the README with tool-specific install flags and plugin marketplace flows. The high commit activity and recent push (May 27, 2026) indicate active maintenance. No single external trigger is identifiable, but the combination of a new release and the expanding ecosystem of AI coding tools (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, etc.) likely fueled the breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses a universal pain point for developers using AI coding assistants: how to consistently get high-quality, task-specific behavior from agents. The npm installer and tool-specific setup make it immediately useful, and the 1,480+ skill catalog offers broad coverage across development, testing, security, and product tasks.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a practical, ready-to-use skill library that can significantly improve the consistency and quality of AI coding assistant outputs. Developers should install it via npx and explore the bundles and workflows to quickly integrate it into their daily workflow.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rapid star growth signals a strong market demand for structured, reusable agentic skills. This trend suggests that the future of AI-assisted development will rely on curated skill libraries rather than ad-hoc prompting, and projects like this may become essential infrastructure for AI coding workflows.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, Kiro, OpenCode, or GitHub Copilot who want to improve the reliability and effectiveness of their AI coding assistants with pre-built, installable skills.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The library is community-driven and may contain low-quality or outdated skills. Users should review skills before use, especially for security-sensitive tasks. The rapid growth could lead to maintenance challenges or skill bloat.

projects.name: code-review-graph
projects.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI that builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, achieving 38x to 528x token reduction on reviews.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens by re-reading large parts of the codebase on review tasks. code-review-graph builds a structural map using Tree-sitter, tracks changes incrementally, and provides precise context via MCP, reducing token consumption dramatically.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 17,511 stars, a 24h star delta of 107, a three-day average of 70, and an acceleration of 1.5 (medium). It has a recent release within 30 days and a commit within 7 days. These metrics indicate sustained high interest and active development, making it a hot project for AI-assisted coding workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The trigger is unclear from the provided data, but the sustained star growth and recent commits suggest ongoing improvements, possibly new platform integrations or performance enhancements.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a critical pain point for developers using AI coding assistants: token waste and context overload. The dramatic token reductions (38x to 528x) and support for many platforms (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) make it highly appealing.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Integrating code-review-graph into your AI coding workflow can drastically reduce token usage and improve review accuracy. It is easy to install and supports incremental updates, making it practical for large monorepos.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, graph-based context management for AI coding tools. Developers should consider adopting such tools to reduce costs and improve AI response quality, and tool builders may want to integrate similar graph-based context retrieval.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) who work on large codebases or monorepos and want to reduce token consumption and improve review accuracy.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new and may have limitations in search quality (MRR 0.35) and flow detection (33% recall). It requires Python 3.10+ and may not support all languages equally. The 100 open issues indicate active development but also potential bugs.

projects.name: ruflo
projects.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features    enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration

llmSummary.oneLiner: Ruflo is a multi-agent orchestration platform for Claude Code that enables coordinated swarms, self-learning memory, and federated agent communication across machines.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Claude Code agents operate in isolation with no shared memory, coordination, or ability to collaborate across sessions or machines. Ruflo solves this by providing a full orchestration layer — swarms, persistent memory, RAG, federation, and enterprise security — so agents work as a cohesive system.
llmSummary.whyNow: Ruflo is experiencing a sudden breakout with 726 stars in 24 hours, a 2.2x acceleration ratio, and 55,883 total stars. The three-day average star delta of 325.5 and yesterday's 310 stars indicate sustained high velocity. This level of attention suggests a major release or community event driving rapid adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project was recently renamed from 'Claude Flow' to 'Ruflo' and has a new release within 30 days. The README mentions a summit in Budapest (June 2–3, 2026) and a UI beta at flo.ruv.io. The combination of rebranding, new features (33 plugins, federation, self-learning), and active promotion likely triggered the breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Ruflo addresses a critical gap in the Claude Code ecosystem: multi-agent coordination. With 33 plugins covering orchestration, memory, security, testing, and domain-specific tasks, it offers a comprehensive solution. The low-friction install (npx ruflo init) and Claude Code plugin path lower the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Ruflo is the most ambitious agent orchestration platform for Claude Code. Developers should evaluate it if they need multi-agent workflows, persistent memory, or cross-machine federation. The plugin architecture allows incremental adoption.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals that the market for agent orchestration is maturing — developers want more than single-agent tools. Ruflo's sudden breakout indicates strong demand for coordinated, memory-enabled agent systems. The focus on Claude Code suggests Anthropic's ecosystem is becoming a primary platform for agentic AI.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Claude Code users building multi-agent systems, AI engineers needing swarm coordination, teams requiring federated agent communication, and developers seeking enterprise-grade agent memory and security.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very new (rebranded recently) with 607 open issues, suggesting rapid development but potential instability. The README contains marketing language and unverified claims (e.g., '22.2M+ ecosystem downloads'). The dependency on Claude Code limits portability. The sudden breakout may be partially driven by hype.

projects.name: rtk
projects.description: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies

llmSummary.oneLiner: A Rust-based CLI proxy that slashes LLM token consumption by 60-90% by filtering and compressing command outputs before they reach AI coding agents.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents like Claude Code burn through tokens on verbose command outputs (e.g., git status, test logs), driving up costs and context limits. rtk intercepts shell commands and returns only the essential information, reducing token usage dramatically with minimal overhead.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 55,414 stars and a sustained 485 stars/day average over the past week, rtk is experiencing a prolonged surge in developer attention. The 24-hour delta of 683 stars and an acceleration ratio of 1.4 indicate strong, steady growth rather than a fleeting spike. This project is riding the wave of cost-conscious AI adoption, where every token saved translates directly to lower API bills.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent star velocity and recent release (within 30 days) suggest ongoing improvements and word-of-mouth adoption among developers using AI coding tools.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Developers using Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, and similar tools are actively seeking ways to reduce token costs. rtk offers a zero-dependency, low-overhead solution that integrates seamlessly via shell hooks, making it an immediate productivity win. The 60-90% token savings are compelling enough to drive viral adoption within the AI coding community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding agents, rtk can cut your token consumption by up to 90% with a single binary install. It's especially valuable for teams running frequent test suites, git operations, or large file reads in their AI workflows.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward token-aware tooling in the AI development stack. As LLM costs remain a concern, developers will increasingly adopt middleware that optimizes context usage. rtk's approach — intercepting at the shell level — is a pattern we'll likely see replicated for other AI integrations.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, etc.) who want to reduce API costs and improve context efficiency, especially those working on large codebases with frequent command execution.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1,001 open issues, which may indicate growing pains or feature requests outpacing development. The hook-based approach only works with Bash tool calls, not built-in agent tools like Read or Grep, limiting its scope. Additionally, the name collision with 'Rust Type Kit' on crates.io could cause installation confusion.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Turn any codebase, knowledge base, or docs into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers joining large codebases struggle to understand architecture and business logic. This tool provides an interactive knowledge graph with guided tours, semantic search, and diff impact analysis to accelerate onboarding and code comprehension.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded in popularity with 38,517 stars, a 24h star delta of 6,337, a three-day average of 5,140, and an acceleration of 1.2 (medium). It has a recent release and a commit within the last 7 days, indicating active development and strong community interest.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The trigger is unclear from the provided data, but the sustained high star velocity and recent commits suggest ongoing feature additions or viral adoption among AI coding tool users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI) by providing a visual, searchable knowledge graph that makes large codebases understandable. The multi-platform support and easy installation lower the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This tool can dramatically reduce onboarding time for new team members and help existing developers quickly grasp complex codebases. It also supports knowledge base analysis, making it useful for documentation and wiki exploration.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The trend signals a growing demand for AI-assisted code comprehension tools that go beyond simple chat. Developers want structured, visual representations of codebases that integrate seamlessly with their existing AI workflows. This could become a standard plugin for AI coding agents.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Software developers, engineering managers, technical leads, and anyone working with large or unfamiliar codebases. Also useful for teams using AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, and Gemini CLI.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on LLM-based analysis which may produce inaccurate summaries for complex or poorly documented code. The knowledge graph is stored as JSON and may become large for huge monorepos, requiring git-lfs. The tool is still relatively new and may have undiscovered bugs or limitations.

hotProjects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
hotProjects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, engineered around prefix-cache stability to keep token costs low across long sessions.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Generic AI coding agents waste tokens on repeated context re-processing. Reasonix locks the prefix cache so that long-running sessions maintain >99% cache hit rates, slashing API costs by ~80% compared to uncached usage.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 3,097 stars in the last 24 hours and 1,922 stars yesterday, with a 7-day average delta of 1,515.5 and an acceleration ratio of 2 (medium). This is a sudden breakout — the project crossed 11,987 stars with 685 forks and 286 open issues, indicating explosive community interest. The velocity badges (Top 2 in Agents, Top 3 in LLMs, Top 3 in CLI) confirm it's one of the fastest-growing repos in its category.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and the detailed README with benchmarks showing 99.82% cache hit and cost savings likely drove a wave of adoption. The addition of a desktop client prerelease and QQ channel integration may have also expanded the user base.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It offers a practical, cost-saving solution for developers who use DeepSeek models extensively. The focus on prefix-cache stability is a unique selling point that directly addresses a pain point (high API costs) without sacrificing functionality. The polished CLI, extensive configuration, and community features (Discord, QQ) lower the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use DeepSeek models for coding tasks, Reasonix can dramatically reduce your token spend while maintaining a full-featured agent loop. The architecture is worth studying for anyone building cache-aware LLM applications.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward model-specific optimizations in AI tooling. Rather than building generic agents that work with any LLM, Reasonix goes all-in on DeepSeek's prefix-cache mechanic, achieving cost efficiencies that generic frameworks cannot match. Developers should consider whether their workflows can benefit from similar cache-aware designs.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers who use DeepSeek models for coding assistance, especially those running long interactive sessions or batch processing. Also relevant for teams looking to reduce API costs without switching away from DeepSeek.
llmSummary.riskNotes: DeepSeek-only — if you switch models, Reasonix loses its core advantage. The project is relatively new (11.9k stars but 286 open issues), so stability and feature completeness may still be evolving. The desktop client is prerelease and not code-signed, which may deter some users.

hotProjects.name: code-review-graph

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI that builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, achieving 38x to 528x token reduction on reviews.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens by re-reading large parts of the codebase on review tasks. code-review-graph builds a structural map using Tree-sitter, tracks changes incrementally, and provides precise context via MCP, reducing token consumption dramatically.

llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Integrating code-review-graph into your AI coding workflow can drastically reduce token usage and improve review accuracy. It is easy to install and supports incremental updates, making it practical for large monorepos.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, graph-based context management for AI coding tools. Developers should consider adopting such tools to reduce costs and improve AI response quality, and tool builders may want to integrate similar graph-based context retrieval.

hotProjects.name: rtk
hotProjects.description: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies

llmSummary.oneLiner: A Rust-based CLI proxy that slashes LLM token consumption by 60-90% by filtering and compressing command outputs before they reach AI coding agents.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents like Claude Code burn through tokens on verbose command outputs (e.g., git status, test logs), driving up costs and context limits. rtk intercepts shell commands and returns only the essential information, reducing token usage dramatically with minimal overhead.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 55,414 stars and a sustained 485 stars/day average over the past week, rtk is experiencing a prolonged surge in developer attention. The 24-hour delta of 683 stars and an acceleration ratio of 1.4 indicate strong, steady growth rather than a fleeting spike. This project is riding the wave of cost-conscious AI adoption, where every token saved translates directly to lower API bills.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent star velocity and recent release (within 30 days) suggest ongoing improvements and word-of-mouth adoption among developers using AI coding tools.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Developers using Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, and similar tools are actively seeking ways to reduce token costs. rtk offers a zero-dependency, low-overhead solution that integrates seamlessly via shell hooks, making it an immediate productivity win. The 60-90% token savings are compelling enough to drive viral adoption within the AI coding community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding agents, rtk can cut your token consumption by up to 90% with a single binary install. It's especially valuable for teams running frequent test suites, git operations, or large file reads in their AI workflows.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward token-aware tooling in the AI development stack. As LLM costs remain a concern, developers will increasingly adopt middleware that optimizes context usage. rtk's approach — intercepting at the shell level — is a pattern we'll likely see replicated for other AI integrations.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, etc.) who want to reduce API costs and improve context efficiency, especially those working on large codebases with frequent command execution.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1,001 open issues, which may indicate growing pains or feature requests outpacing development. The hook-based approach only works with Bash tool calls, not built-in agent tools like Read or Grep, limiting its scope. Additionally, the name collision with 'Rust Type Kit' on crates.io could cause installation confusion.

modelDemoSignals.summary: Parameter-efficient finetuning (PEFT) has become the standard approach for adapting large language models, yet evaluations largely emphasize downstream accuracy while overlooking the retention of pretrained capabilities. We argue that PEFT should be assessed through the stability-plasticity dilemma: the trade-off between target-task adaptation and resistance to forgetting. We introduce PEFT-Arena, a benchmark that jointly measures downstream performance and general capability retention. Across methods, we find distinct stability-plasticity profiles; under comparable parameter budgets, orthogonal finetuning achieves the most favorable Pareto frontier. To explain these differences, we analyze PEFT updates from two geometric perspectives. In weight space, spectral analysis reveals how parameterizations interact with the pretrained singular-value structure. In activation space, retention metrics show whether finetuning preserves or distorts general-capability representations, with forgetting linked to non-isometric representation distortion. Finally, an analysis shows that final SFT checkpoints often overshoot a better target-retention operating point. Inspired by this, we present case studies of a post-hoc improvement with path-wise rewinding.
modelDemoSignals.author: Yangyi Huang, Ruotian Peng, Zeju Qiu, Jiale Kang, Yandong Wen, Bernhard Schölkopf, Weiyang Liu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have become increasingly useful computational models of human language processing, but it remains unclear whether vision-language learning makes text representations more human-like during natural reading. Here, we address this question by comparing tightly matched LLM and vision-language model (VLM) pairs under a strictly text-only setting, allowing us to isolate the effect of multimodal training history from online visual input or cross-modal fusion. We evaluate model alignment with a human natural-reading dataset that includes whole-cortex fMRI responses and synchronized eye-tracking saccades. Our findings demonstrate that multimodal pretraining may not confer a uniform, global advantage in human alignment during natural reading, indicating that language-internal representations remain the key factor for modeling human text processing. However, the VLM advantage could emerge more selectively when sentences contain stronger visual semantic content, with converging evidence from both fMRI and eye-movement alignments. Together, our findings provide a controlled in silico framework for testing how visual learning history shapes model-human alignment of language processing, suggesting that multimodal pretraining contributes selectively rather than globally to human-like language representations during natural reading.
modelDemoSignals.author: Jinzhou Wu, Zhengwu Ma, Jixing Li, Baoping Tang, Zitong Lu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment

modelDemoSignals.title: Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search
modelDemoSignals.description: Search has been proposed as an effective method for self-improving language models and agentic systems, both for post-training sample generation and for inference. However, widely used methods such as best-of-N sampling and tree search face two fundamental limitations: they are guided by sparse verification signals, and they construct candidates primarily through autoregressive expansion, restricting exploration to regions with substantial model probability mass. To address these, we propose Bid
modelDemoSignals.summary: Search has been proposed as an effective method for self-improving language models and agentic systems, both for post-training sample generation and for inference. However, widely used methods such as best-of-N sampling and tree search face two fundamental limitations: they are guided by sparse verification signals, and they construct candidates primarily through autoregressive expansion, restricting exploration to regions with substantial model probability mass. To address these, we propose Bidirectional Evolutionary Search (BES), a search framework that couples forward candidate evolution with backward goal decomposition. In the forward search, BES augments standard expansion with evolution operators that recombine partial trajectories to generate candidates that are difficult to obtain from a single model rollout. In the backward search, BES recursively decomposes the original task into checkable subgoals, producing dense intermediate feedback that guides forward search. We provide theoretical motivation showing that candidates generated by expansion-only search are confined to a narrow entropy shell while evolutionary operators can escape it, and that backward search can exponentially reduce the number of required samples to find a correct answer. Experiments show that on challenging post-training tasks where mainstream post-training algorithms fail to improve, BES enables consistent gains, and on three open problem solving benchmarks at inference time, BES outperforms existing open-source frameworks in both average and best-case performance. Code and trained models are available at https://github.com/Embodied-Minds-Lab/BES.
modelDemoSignals.author: Guowei Xu, Zhenting Qi, Huangyuan Su, Weirui Ye, Himabindu Lakkaraju, Sham M. Kakade, Yilun Du
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, benchmark, agentic

modelDemoSignals.summary: Class-Incremental Learning (CIL) is important in building real-world learning systems. In CLIP-based CIL, the model performs classification by comparing similarity between visual and textual embeddings obtained from template prompts, e.g., ``a photo of a [CLASS]''. This seemingly monolithic matching process can be decomposed into two conceptually distinct stages: attribute extraction and attribute aggregation. For example, a model may recognize cat using attributes such as fur texture and whiskers. When learning a new class like car, the model must extract additional attributes like wheels and adjust how they are aggregated in the shared representation space. However, since only data from the current task is available, incremental updates can bias both attribute extraction and aggregation toward new classes, leading to catastrophic forgetting. Therefore, we propose AREA for attribute extraction and aggregation in CLIP-based CIL. To stabilize extraction, we anchor class-level visual and textual attributes on the hyperspherical embedding space via principal geodesic analysis. To stabilize aggregation, we learn lightweight task-specific experts with scoring and residual refinement, regularized by a variational information bottleneck objective. During inference, we perform routing over task attribute manifolds via optimal transport for more concise prediction. Experiments show that AREA consistently outperforms SOTA methods. Code is available at https://github.com/LAMDA-CL/ICML2026-AREA.
modelDemoSignals.author: Zhen-Hao Xie, Yu-Cheng Shi, Da-Wei Zhou

modelDemoSignals.title: Calibrating Conservatism for Scalable Oversight
modelDemoSignals.description: Agentic AI systems capable of autonomous planning and extended environmental interaction pose a fundamental control problem: how can humans maintain meaningful oversight of systems that may exceed their own capabilities? Existing approaches to scalable oversight rely on complex assumptions, remain largely heuristic, or lack practical methods for sequential settings with statistical guarantees. modelDemoSignals.summary: Agentic AI systems capable of autonomous planning and extended environmental interaction pose a fundamental control problem: how can humans maintain meaningful oversight of systems that may exceed their own capabilities? Existing approaches to scalable oversight rely on complex assumptions, remain largely heuristic, or lack practical methods for sequential settings with statistical guarantees. We introduce Calibrated Collective Oversight (CCO), which aggregates diverse auxiliary scoring functions into a penalty measuring deviation from a conservative baseline. Inspired by Attainable Utility Preservation, CCO enables collective conservatism: actions face a penalty proportional to overseer concern, so high-utility actions are still selected when overseers find them unobjectionable and overridden only when concern accumulates. CCO calibrates this conservatism online using Conformal Decision Theory, ensuring that undesirable outcomes remain below a user-specified target threshold with finite-time bounds and no distributional assumptions. On a modified version of SWE-bench, weaker overseers successfully constrain an adversarially misaligned stronger agent; on MACHIAVELLI, CCO substantially reduces ethical violations while preserving reward. In both settings, empirical violation rates closely match the specified targets, as predicted by the theory.
modelDemoSignals.author: William Overman, Mohsen Bayati

aihotHighlights.title: 英伟达推出 AI 框架 Polar，让 Codex 跑分暴涨 594.74%
aihotHighlights.summary: 英伟达研究团队开源了智能体强化学习框架 Polar。该框架无需重写现有智能体执行框架（如 Codex CLI、Claude Code、Qwen Code、Pi），通过在模型 API 边界放置智能体来接入 GRPO 训练。实验显示，基于 Qwen3.5-4B 模型，Polar 将 Codex 在 SWE-Bench Verified 上的 pass@1 分数从 3.8% 提升至 26.4%（增涨 594.74%）。效率上，其 prefix_merging 技术将训练步骤从 1185 次降至 218 次，速度提升约 5.39 倍，GPU 平均利用率从 20.4% 升至 87.7%。

aihotHighlights.title: 用好 Coding Agent，重点是两头，尤其是开头的部分，如果一开始就走偏了后面怎么改都改不好。
aihotHighlights.summary: 用好 Coding Agent 的关键在于初始规划。方法是先将需求整理后，用最强模型（如 GPT-5.5、Claude Opus 4.7）分别在 Codex、Claude Code、Cursor 的 Plan 模式下生成设计方案，选择最优方案并借鉴其他版本。对于复杂计划，可将其拆分为多个 Phases 并明确要求与验证标准，形成 Markdown 文档。执行时按 Phases 进行，并辅以人工审核纠偏。最后的代码审核（Code Review）用 GPT-5.5 审核代码质量与设计符合度即可。应避免让多个智能体交叉 Review，否则可能导致代码越改越多。

aihotHighlights.title: 社会科学中的编码智能体
aihotHighlights.summary: 一项针对1260名定量社会科学家的调查显示，虽然81%的受访者用过AI聊天机器人，但仅有20%将Claude Code、Codex等编码智能体常规应用于工作。采用率存在显著差异：以男性名字命名的研究者使用率是女性研究者的两倍；顶尖大学研究者可能性高出40%。用户产出更多工作论文和基金申请，但这可能反映早期采用者自身差异。研究者对AI助力撰写可发表论文更乐观，但对重塑整个社会科学领域持保留态度。这是一项初步调查，更深入研究仍在进行中。

aihotHighlights.title: 使用大语言模型保障源代码安全
aihotHighlights.summary: 本文分享了使用 Claude Opus 构建威胁模型、发现代码漏洞并进行验证、分类和修复的最佳实践。其核心流程是一个六步循环：威胁建模、沙箱隔离、漏洞发现、验证、分类和修复。作者指出，漏洞发现现在易于并行化，瓶颈已转移到后续的验证与处理阶段。以他们对开源软件的扫描为例，截至2026年5月22日已披露1，596个漏洞，其中97个已修补。指南建议结合代码库文档和专家访谈来构建准确的威胁模型，以降低误报，提升发现的可利用性。

aihotHighlights.title: Cognition成为全球最大独立智能体实验室
aihotHighlights.summary: Cognition宣布已成为全球最大的独立智能体实验室。公司完成超10亿美元融资，估值达260亿美元，由Lux Capital、General Catalyst等领投。其企业使用量自年初增长超10倍，年化收入增至4.92亿美元。Cognition于两年前推出Devin，定位为首个AI软件工程师。公司强调其拥有多项领先优势，包括首个编码智能体、顶级代码审查能力等，并得到了Peter Thiel的重大投资。

aihotHighlights.title: AI智能体的零信任安全框架
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic 发布了针对企业部署自主 AI 智能体的安全框架，指出前沿大语言模型正将漏洞利用周期从数月压缩至数小时。部署智能体面临双重风险：基础设施易受 AI 加速攻击，且智能体自身具备自主决策与执行能力。文章提出一个三层零信任架构（基础、高级、优化级）及八阶段实施流程，并概述了提示注入、工具投毒、记忆投毒等特有威胁。

items.title: 我认为 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 找到了产品市场契合点
items.summary: Anthropic 与 OpenAI 通过编程智能体找到了产品市场契合点，这导致企业客户成本显著上升。两家公司已于 2026 年 4 月前后调整了企业套餐定价，从原先的高额折扣改为与 API 用量挂钩。Anthropic Enterprise 套餐变为每席位 20 美元/月外加 API 费用，OpenAI Codex 则按 API token 用量计费。同期发布的新模型 GPT-5.5（4月23日）和 Opus 4.7（4月16日）的 API 定价也显著高于前代版本。

llmSummary.whatItIs: Anthropic and OpenAI have shifted enterprise pricing to API-usage-based models, indicating product-market fit for coding agents.
llmSummary.whyNow: Pricing changes in April 2026 and high HN engagement (805 upvotes, 945 comments) signal strong developer interest in the economic shift.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Enterprise developers, CTOs, AI platform engineers, and product managers evaluating AI coding tools.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Enterprise AI costs are rising as vendors move from flat fees to usage-based pricing, impacting budget planning.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Developers should monitor API costs and consider agent efficiency to manage expenses.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Pricing changes may not reflect long-term adoption; HN discussion is interest, not proven enterprise commitment.

items.title: Brew
items.description: Like Claude design for email marketing - Brew is the fastest way to design and send beautiful, on-brand emails and automations that render perfectly in every inbox. Describe a campaign or a multi-step automation in plain English, and Brew builds the whole thing in seconds: copy, design, audience, and logic. Works with any AI agent: paste our docs into OpenClaw, Viktor, Claude, or Lovable. No lock-in: send from Brew or export to your ESP. Free to get started.
items.whyItMatters: Like Claude design for email marketing - Brew is the fastest way to design and send beautiful, on-brand emails and automations that render perfectly in every inbox. Describe a campaign or a multi-step automation in plain English, and Brew builds the whole thing in seconds: copy, design, audience, and logic. Works with any AI agent: paste our docs into OpenClaw, Viktor, Claude, or Lovable. No lock-in: send from Brew or export to your ESP. Free to get started.

items.title: Powabase
items.description: Build AI apps with Postgres, RAG, and agents - Powabase is a backend-as-a-service for AI-native applications, combining Postgres, RAG, agents, memory, workflows, and automation primitives in one platform. It helps agencies and in-house IT teams build new AI apps or add AI automation to existing products without stitching together fragmented infrastructure. Designed to work seamlessly with modern coding agents, Powabase helps teams ship faster while building more robust, token-efficient systems.
items.whyItMatters: Build AI apps with Postgres, RAG, and agents - Powabase is a backend-as-a-service for AI-native applications, combining Postgres, RAG, agents, memory, workflows, and automation primitives in one platform. It helps agencies and in-house IT teams build new AI apps or add AI automation to existing products without stitching together fragmented infrastructure. Designed to work seamlessly with modern coding agents, Powabase helps teams ship faster while building more robust, token-efficient systems.

items.title: Bluedot 2.1
items.description: Record on Apple Watch. Sync with Claude - Bluedot 2.1 brings your real-world conversations into Claude. Record conversations directly from your Apple Watch, then sync them with Claude through MCP. Capture customer calls, hallway chats, interviews, coffee meetings, and in-person conversations, without a laptop or meeting bot. Bluedot turns every conversation into searchable, AI-ready context that Claude can summarize, search, and act on.

items.title: Rezonant
items.description: Talk, spec, ship: get your product ideas into production - Rezonant helps product teams turn messy ideas into code-ready specs, tickets, and engineering tasks. Collaborate with PMs, engineers, designers, and AI agents in one shared workspace. Ground decisions in your actual codebase, keep everyone aligned on the same version, and create work that humans and coding agents can confidently ship.

trendEntities.title: 我认为 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 找到了产品市场契合点
normalizedKeys: title:我认为 anthropic 和 openai 找到了产品市场契合点
title:i think anthropic and openai have found product market fit

items.title: 我认为 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 找到了产品市场契合点
items.summary: Anthropic 与 OpenAI 通过编程智能体找到了产品市场契合点，这导致企业客户成本显著上升。两家公司已于 2026 年 4 月前后调整了企业套餐定价，从原先的高额折扣改为与 API 用量挂钩。Anthropic Enterprise 套餐变为每席位 20 美元/月外加 API 费用，OpenAI Codex 则按 API token 用量计费。同期发布的新模型 GPT-5.5（4月23日）和 Opus 4.7（4月16日）的 API 定价也显著高于前代版本。

trendEntities.summary: Anthropic 与 OpenAI 通过编程智能体找到了产品市场契合点，这导致企业客户成本显著上升。两家公司已于 2026 年 4 月前后调整了企业套餐定价，从原先的高额折扣改为与 API 用量挂钩。Anthropic Enterprise 套餐变为每席位 20 美元/月外加 API 费用，OpenAI Codex 则按 API token 用量计费。同期发布的新模型 GPT-5.5（4月23日）和 Opus 4.7（4月16日）的 API 定价也显著高于前代版本。
trendEntities.whyItMatters: 同一趋势被 2 个来源、2 条信号捕捉。

llmSummary.whatItIs: Anthropic and OpenAI have shifted enterprise pricing to API-usage-based models, indicating product-market fit for coding agents.
llmSummary.whyNow: Pricing changes in April 2026 and high HN engagement (805 upvotes, 945 comments) signal strong developer interest in the economic shift.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Enterprise developers, CTOs, AI platform engineers, and product managers evaluating AI coding tools.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Enterprise AI costs are rising as vendors move from flat fees to usage-based pricing, impacting budget planning.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Developers should monitor API costs and consider agent efficiency to manage expenses.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Pricing changes may not reflect long-term adoption; HN discussion is interest, not proven enterprise commitment.

trendEntities.title: Brew

items.title: Brew
items.description: Like Claude design for email marketing - Brew is the fastest way to design and send beautiful, on-brand emails and automations that render perfectly in every inbox. Describe a campaign or a multi-step automation in plain English, and Brew builds the whole thing in seconds: copy, design, audience, and logic. Works with any AI agent: paste our docs into OpenClaw, Viktor, Claude, or Lovable. No lock-in: send from Brew or export to your ESP. Free to get started.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Brew is an AI-powered email design and automation tool that generates on-brand emails and multi-step campaigns from natural language descriptions, with export to any ESP.
llmSummary.whyNow: Launched on Product Hunt with 720 upvotes and 119 comments, indicating strong initial interest from the design and marketing community.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Email marketers, product designers, growth teams, and developers building email workflows.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Potential to reduce email design and automation setup time, but early-stage with no production adoption data.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Offers API/docs for integration with AI agents; developers can evaluate for custom email pipeline automation.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Product Hunt votes reflect launch attention, not code quality or production reliability. No GitHub stars or open-source evidence. Claims of 'no lock-in' need verification.

trendEntities.title: Powabase

items.title: Powabase
items.description: Build AI apps with Postgres, RAG, and agents - Powabase is a backend-as-a-service for AI-native applications, combining Postgres, RAG, agents, memory, workflows, and automation primitives in one platform. It helps agencies and in-house IT teams build new AI apps or add AI automation to existing products without stitching together fragmented infrastructure. Designed to work seamlessly with modern coding agents, Powabase helps teams ship faster while building more robust, token-efficient systems.

trendEntities.title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated videos
normalizedKeys: title:youtube to automatically label ai generated videos

items.title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated videos
items.author: nopg

sourceItems.title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated videos
sourceItems.author: nopg

llmSummary.whatItIs: YouTube announced it will require creators to label AI-generated or manipulated content that looks realistic, and will automatically apply labels for certain types of synthetic content.
llmSummary.whyNow: The announcement generated significant developer discussion on Hacker News (782 upvotes, 452 comments), indicating strong interest in the policy's technical implications and enforcement challenges.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Video platform developers, content moderation engineers, AI/ML researchers working on synthetic media detection, and product managers at social media companies.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Sets a precedent for platform responsibility in labeling AI-generated content, potentially influencing policies at other major platforms.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Developers building video platforms or content moderation tools may need to implement similar labeling systems; the discussion highlights technical challenges in automated detection.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Policy details are still evolving; enforcement effectiveness and impact on creator workflows are uncertain. High HN engagement reflects interest but not necessarily adoption.

llmSummary.whyNow: The post gained significant traction on Hacker News (173 upvotes, 53 comments), indicating strong developer curiosity about decentralized, off-grid communication alternatives.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers interested in IoT, LoRa, mesh networking, or resilient communication; product managers exploring decentralized infrastructure; security researchers.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Potential for niche applications in disaster recovery, remote monitoring, and privacy-focused communication; not yet mainstream.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Opportunity to experiment with low-cost hardware and open-source stacks for building resilient networks; APIs and SDKs available for integration.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Hacker News attention does not imply production readiness; projects are early-stage with limited documentation and community support; regulatory constraints on radio frequencies may apply.

sourceItems.author: HelloUsername

llmSummary.whatItIs: DuckDuckGo, a privacy-focused search engine without AI features, saw a 28% increase in visits after Google promoted its AI mode, suggesting user backlash against AI integration in search.
llmSummary.whyNow: The spike occurred immediately after Google's announcement, indicating strong developer and user interest in non-AI alternatives, and the HN discussion (806 votes, 380 comments) shows high community engagement.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Search engine developers, privacy tool builders, product managers at ad-supported platforms, and developers evaluating user sentiment on AI features.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Signals potential market shift toward privacy-first products; may influence product decisions on AI integration for search and other tools.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Developers building search or privacy tools should monitor user sentiment; consider offering non-AI modes or opt-out options.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Single data point from one week; correlation not causation; HN discussion may overrepresent developer sentiment vs general users.

trendEntities.title: crunr

items.title: crunr
items.description: Launch and run any compute job on AWS with 1 command - crunr — run it, ghost it. GPU compute is $1.5/hr. But your real bill looks like this: - Idle time sitting there: $800/mo - Infra team to manage it: $3,000/mo - Failed setups and debugging: days lost - 3am emergency fixes: priceless crunr fixes all of it. $ crunr run train.py --gpu Spins up → runs → terminates. You pay for compute only. Nothing else. No idle bills. No DevOps. No lingering servers. Built for ML researchers, indie AI builders, and startup teams who just want their job to run.

trendEntities.title: Hayley: Your Thinking Companion

items.title: Hayley: Your Thinking Companion

items.description: Prototype hardware visually, export real C++ for engineering - blokdots is a visual tool for building interactive hardware. Export real C++ or JavaScript code, hand off to engineering, or go fully standalone: upload directly to Arduino, no more laptop needed! Connects to ProtoPie, Figma, and Socket.IO for integrated UI + hardware prototyping.

items.title: Ajar
items.description: Lid Angle Sync & Keep Awake for AI Agents on Mac - Ajar makes your Mac respond to what you're doing, not what you remember to press. Lid Angle Sync maps your MacBook's lid angle to screen brightness, keyboard backlight, and system volume. Multiple displays are supported, with per-display offsets. Awake keeps the Mac awake when you're actually working. Built-in rules detect Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI sessions. Add your own for apps, Wi-Fi, disks, power, or a closed-lid clamshell.

trendEntities.title: Chunk sidecars

items.title: Chunk sidecars
items.description: Validate agent-generated code before it ever reaches CI - AI agents write code fast. Validation still happens after the push — by then the context is gone. Chunk sidecars run scoped microbuilds before commit, in a real CI mirror. Auto-detects your stack. ~27s average vs ~5 min billable compute for a full run. 3x–5x fewer tokens in retry loops. If something fails, the agent iterates before anything reaches shared CI. Run chunk init. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or custom agents. Free for all CircleCI users.

trendEntities.title: AgenticCalling AI

items.title: AgenticCalling AI
items.description: Give your AI the power to make phone calls - Give your AI a dedicated phone number and the power to make calls. AgenticCalling lets Claude, ChatGPT, OpenClaw or any custom agent execute unscripted, goal-oriented calls. We handle the messy infra - Numbers, A2P, IVRs, voicemails, & retries. Your agent dials out, navigates conversations, and returns clean JSON data. Built for action: rate shopping, negotiations, & real-world execution. Just say: “Hey Claude, call my mom at +1-786786786 and tell her I love her!”

trendEntities.title: 英伟达推出 AI 框架 Polar，让 Codex 跑分暴涨 594.74%
normalizedKeys: title:英伟达推出 ai 框架 polar 让 codex 跑分暴涨 594 74

items.title: 英伟达推出 AI 框架 Polar，让 Codex 跑分暴涨 594.74%
items.summary: 英伟达研究团队开源了智能体强化学习框架 Polar。该框架无需重写现有智能体执行框架（如 Codex CLI、Claude Code、Qwen Code、Pi），通过在模型 API 边界放置智能体来接入 GRPO 训练。实验显示，基于 Qwen3.5-4B 模型，Polar 将 Codex 在 SWE-Bench Verified 上的 pass@1 分数从 3.8% 提升至 26.4%（增涨 594.74%）。效率上，其 prefix_merging 技术将训练步骤从 1185 次降至 218 次，速度提升约 5.39 倍，GPU 平均利用率从 20.4% 升至 87.7%。

trendEntities.title: 人民日报专访华为何庭波：今年秋季的新麒麟手机芯片，性能等相比去年是"跳跃性"提升
normalizedKeys: title:人民日报专访华为何庭波 今年秋季的新麒麟手机芯片 性能等相比去年是 跳跃性 提升

items.title: 人民日报专访华为何庭波：今年秋季的新麒麟手机芯片，性能等相比去年是"跳跃性"提升
items.summary: 华为何庭波提出半导体新演进路径"韬（τ）定律"，以"时间缩微"（如逻辑折叠）替代"几何缩微"作为新指导原则。她表示，过去6年华为已基于此自主研发381款芯片。今年秋季将发布新的麒麟手机芯片，这是首个完整的"韬芯片"，其性能、集成度相比去年是"跳跃性"提升。

trendEntities.title: 用好 Coding Agent，重点是两头，尤其是开头的部分，如果一开始就走偏了后面怎么改都改不好。
normalizedKeys: title:用好 coding agent 重点是两头 尤其是开头的部分 如果一开始就走偏了后面怎么改都改不好

items.title: 用好 Coding Agent，重点是两头，尤其是开头的部分，如果一开始就走偏了后面怎么改都改不好。
items.summary: 用好 Coding Agent 的关键在于初始规划。方法是先将需求整理后，用最强模型（如 GPT-5.5、Claude Opus 4.7）分别在 Codex、Claude Code、Cursor 的 Plan 模式下生成设计方案，选择最优方案并借鉴其他版本。对于复杂计划，可将其拆分为多个 Phases 并明确要求与验证标准，形成 Markdown 文档。执行时按 Phases 进行，并辅以人工审核纠偏。最后的代码审核（Code Review）用 GPT-5.5 审核代码质量与设计符合度即可。应避免让多个智能体交叉 Review，否则可能导致代码越改越多。

trendEntities.title: 社会科学中的编码智能体
normalizedKeys: title:社会科学中的编码智能体

items.title: 社会科学中的编码智能体
items.summary: 一项针对1260名定量社会科学家的调查显示，虽然81%的受访者用过AI聊天机器人，但仅有20%将Claude Code、Codex等编码智能体常规应用于工作。采用率存在显著差异：以男性名字命名的研究者使用率是女性研究者的两倍；顶尖大学研究者可能性高出40%。用户产出更多工作论文和基金申请，但这可能反映早期采用者自身差异。研究者对AI助力撰写可发表论文更乐观，但对重塑整个社会科学领域持保留态度。这是一项初步调查，更深入研究仍在进行中。

trendEntities.title: 使用大语言模型保障源代码安全
normalizedKeys: title:使用大语言模型保障源代码安全

items.title: 使用大语言模型保障源代码安全
items.summary: 本文分享了使用 Claude Opus 构建威胁模型、发现代码漏洞并进行验证、分类和修复的最佳实践。其核心流程是一个六步循环：威胁建模、沙箱隔离、漏洞发现、验证、分类和修复。作者指出，漏洞发现现在易于并行化，瓶颈已转移到后续的验证与处理阶段。以他们对开源软件的扫描为例，截至2026年5月22日已披露1，596个漏洞，其中97个已修补。指南建议结合代码库文档和专家访谈来构建准确的威胁模型，以降低误报，提升发现的可利用性。

trendEntities.title: Cognition成为全球最大独立智能体实验室
normalizedKeys: title:cognition成为全球最大独立智能体实验室

items.title: Cognition成为全球最大独立智能体实验室
items.summary: Cognition宣布已成为全球最大的独立智能体实验室。公司完成超10亿美元融资，估值达260亿美元，由Lux Capital、General Catalyst等领投。其企业使用量自年初增长超10倍，年化收入增至4.92亿美元。Cognition于两年前推出Devin，定位为首个AI软件工程师。公司强调其拥有多项领先优势，包括首个编码智能体、顶级代码审查能力等，并得到了Peter Thiel的重大投资。

trendEntities.title: Web 更新

items.title: Web 更新
items.summary: 对话模式在文本和语音输入方面进行了改进。语音会话开始时，可访问用户的图像提示、风格参考、侧边栏设置和最近任务。图像提示功能现可从托盘和侧边栏直接使用。在语音提交过程中，托盘中的图像将保持不变，直至用户手动移除。

trendEntities.title: AI智能体的零信任安全框架
normalizedKeys: title:ai智能体的零信任安全框架

items.title: AI智能体的零信任安全框架
items.summary: Anthropic 发布了针对企业部署自主 AI 智能体的安全框架，指出前沿大语言模型正将漏洞利用周期从数月压缩至数小时。部署智能体面临双重风险：基础设施易受 AI 加速攻击，且智能体自身具备自主决策与执行能力。文章提出一个三层零信任架构（基础、高级、优化级）及八阶段实施流程，并概述了提示注入、工具投毒、记忆投毒等特有威胁。

trendEntities.title: Google Pay 最新更新

items.title: Google Pay 最新更新
items.summary: Google Pay 正向"智能体商务"演进，推出了通用商务协议和新的 MCP 服务器，允许 AI 智能体管理集成与分析趋势。Android 平台更新引入了动态回调以支持快速结账，并通过 WebView 将支付功能扩展至社交媒体应用。此外，平台还推出了跨设备生物认证和新的交易信号，旨在帮助商家减少流程摩擦。

trendEntities.title: 通过零信任聚合实现的隐私分析
normalizedKeys: title:通过零信任聚合实现的隐私分析

items.title: 通过零信任聚合实现的隐私分析
items.summary: Google Research 推出了一种新的隐私分析解决方案。该方案结合了一种新的密码学安全聚合协议与可信执行环境（TEE）的透明性，旨在实现前沿的隐私与安全保证。其核心是基于零信任原则，通过密码学与硬件保护的结合，确保系统仅能获取群体的匿名化聚合洞察。

trendEntities.title: ITBench-AA：前沿大模型在首个智能体企业IT任务基准测试中得分均低于50%
normalizedKeys: title:itbench aa 前沿大模型在首个智能体企业it任务基准测试中得分均低于50

items.title: ITBench-AA：前沿大模型在首个智能体企业IT任务基准测试中得分均低于50%
items.summary: 由Artificial Analysis和IBM推出的ITBench-AA SRE基准测试显示，所有前沿大模型得分均未超过50%。Claude Opus 4.7（自适应推理，最大努力）以47%领先，GPT-5.5（xhigh）和Qwen3.7 Max分别得46%和42%。该测试包含59个需要通过Shell命令调查Kubernetes事件快照并提交根因诊断的智能体任务。关键发现是模型推理轮次差异近3倍，但更长的轨迹并不转化为更高准确率，过度调查的模型会因提交误报而受罚。在成本方面，开源模型Gemma 4 31B（Reasoning）以每任务$0.14的成本获得37%得分，优于成本更高但得分更低的闭源模型。

trendEntities.title: Fast， faster， Qwen. 🚀

items.title: Fast， faster， Qwen. 🚀
items.summary: Qwen3.5在TokenSpeed推理引擎上，针对智能体工作负载达到了创纪录的580 tokens per second （tps）速度。这一成果由通义千问推理团队、lightseekorg Foundation TokenSpeed团队、NVIDIA及Mooncake团队共同实现，并采用了tri_dao的FlashAttention-4 （FA4） 优化。此里程碑标志着开源大语言模型推理性能的边界得到了推动，相关详情可查阅PyTorch社区博客。

trendEntities.title: 与Google搜索产品副总裁Robby Stein的访谈：AI原生搜索时代
normalizedKeys: title:与google搜索产品副总裁robby stein的访谈 ai原生搜索时代

items.title: 与Google搜索产品副总裁Robby Stein的访谈：AI原生搜索时代
items.summary: 本文记录了与Google搜索产品副总裁Robby Stein在Google I/O的访谈，核心探讨Google Search向"AI原生"模式的重大转变。讨论话题包括AI Mode是进化还是重塑、如何将复杂问题拆解为多轮搜索、AI搜索的高运行成本、Google TPU及基础设施的优势、AI时代搜索量不减反增的原因，以及优质AI回答与出版商流量之间的张力。访谈还涉及Google决定展示哪些信息源与链接的逻辑，并围绕一个核心问题展开：如果Google直接给出答案，传统的基于链接的网页生态将走向何方？

sourceItems.title: 与Google搜索产品副总裁Robby Stein的访谈：AI原生搜索时代
sourceItems.summary: 本文记录了与Google搜索产品副总裁Robby Stein在Google I/O的访谈，核心探讨Google Search向"AI原生"模式的重大转变。讨论话题包括AI Mode是进化还是重塑、如何将复杂问题拆解为多轮搜索、AI搜索的高运行成本、Google TPU及基础设施的优势、AI时代搜索量不减反增的原因，以及优质AI回答与出版商流量之间的张力。访谈还涉及Google决定展示哪些信息源与链接的逻辑，并围绕一个核心问题展开：如果Google直接给出答案，传统的基于链接的网页生态将走向何方？

trendEntities.title: Perplexity开源Unigram分词器降低CPU占用

items.title: Perplexity开源Unigram分词器降低CPU占用
items.summary: 我们开源了重新构建的Unigram分词器，可将CPU占用降低5-6倍。 小型重排序器和嵌入模型在GPU上运行时间仅为个位数毫秒，使得CPU分词成为总延迟的重要组成部分。 http：//github.com/perplexityai/pplx-garden

trendEntities.title: Runway 推出 Model Context Protocol 服务器
normalizedKeys: title:runway 推出 model context protocol 服务器

items.title: Runway 推出 Model Context Protocol 服务器
items.summary: Runway 正式推出 Runway MCP 服务器，允许任何兼容 MCP 的 AI 智能体（如 Claude、ChatGPT、Cursor）在对话界面中直接生成图像与视频，无需切换工作流。该服务器接入了 Runway 最新的多款 SOTA 模型，包括 Gen-4.5、Seedance 2.0、GPT Image 2、Kling 3.0 及 Nano Banana Pro。其应用场景涵盖为产品制作营销视频、批量生成网站视觉素材、创作角色广告以及在应用开发中集成视觉内容。用户设置简便，通过 runwayml.com/mcp 添加服务器并登录现有 Runway 账户即可使用，无需单独申请 API 密钥。

trendEntities.title: 教皇没对AGI上头

items.title: 教皇没对AGI上头
items.summary: 教皇 Leo XIV 发布题为《Magnifica Humanitas》的通谕，警告人工智能的使用绝非纯粹技术问题，当其进入影响人类生活的过程时，便触及权利、机会、地位与自由。通谕发布时，Anthropic 联合创始人 Christopher Olah 出席。文件引发了科技界内外的广泛反应。

items.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
items.description: Parameter-efficient finetuning (PEFT) has become the standard approach for adapting large language models, yet evaluations largely emphasize downstream accuracy while overlooking the retention of pretrained capabilities. We argue that PEFT should be assessed through the stability-plasticity dilemma: the trade-off between target-task adaptation and resistance to forgetting. We introduce PEFT-Arena, a benchmark that jointly measures downstream performance and general capability retention. Across m
items.summary: Parameter-efficient finetuning (PEFT) has become the standard approach for adapting large language models, yet evaluations largely emphasize downstream accuracy while overlooking the retention of pretrained capabilities. We argue that PEFT should be assessed through the stability-plasticity dilemma: the trade-off between target-task adaptation and resistance to forgetting. We introduce PEFT-Arena, a benchmark that jointly measures downstream performance and general capability retention. Across methods, we find distinct stability-plasticity profiles; under comparable parameter budgets, orthogonal finetuning achieves the most favorable Pareto frontier. To explain these differences, we analyze PEFT updates from two geometric perspectives. In weight space, spectral analysis reveals how parameterizations interact with the pretrained singular-value structure. In activation space, retention metrics show whether finetuning preserves or distorts general-capability representations, with forgetting linked to non-isometric representation distortion. Finally, an analysis shows that final SFT checkpoints often overshoot a better target-retention operating point. Inspired by this, we present case studies of a post-hoc improvement with path-wise rewinding.
items.author: Yangyi Huang, Ruotian Peng, Zeju Qiu, Jiale Kang, Yandong Wen, Bernhard Schölkopf, Weiyang Liu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, benchmark, evaluation

items.title: VLMs May Not Globally Enhance Human Alignment over LLMs During Natural Reading
items.description: Large language models (LLMs) have become increasingly useful computational models of human language processing, but it remains unclear whether vision-language learning makes text representations more human-like during natural reading. items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have become increasingly useful computational models of human language processing, but it remains unclear whether vision-language learning makes text representations more human-like during natural reading. Here, we address this question by comparing tightly matched LLM and vision-language model (VLM) pairs under a strictly text-only setting, allowing us to isolate the effect of multimodal training history from online visual input or cross-modal fusion. We evaluate model alignment with a human natural-reading dataset that includes whole-cortex fMRI responses and synchronized eye-tracking saccades. Our findings demonstrate that multimodal pretraining may not confer a uniform, global advantage in human alignment during natural reading, indicating that language-internal representations remain the key factor for modeling human text processing. However, the VLM advantage could emerge more selectively when sentences contain stronger visual semantic content, with converging evidence from both fMRI and eye-movement alignments. Together, our findings provide a controlled in silico framework for testing how visual learning history shapes model-human alignment of language processing, suggesting that multimodal pretraining contributes selectively rather than globally to human-like language representations during natural reading.
items.author: Jinzhou Wu, Zhengwu Ma, Jixing Li, Baoping Tang, Zitong Lu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment

trendEntities.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have become increasingly useful computational models of human language processing, but it remains unclear whether vision-language learning makes text representations more human-like during natural reading. Here, we address this question by comparing tightly matched LLM and vision-language model (VLM) pairs under a strictly text-only setting, allowing us to isolate the effect of multimodal training history from online visual input or cross-modal fusion. However, the VLM advantage could emerge more selectively when sentences contain stronger visual semantic content, with converging evidence from both fMRI and eye-movement alignments. Together, our findings provide a controlled in silico framework for testing how visual learning history shapes model-human alignment of language processing, suggesting that multimodal pretraining contributes selectively rather than globally to human-like language representations during natural reading.

items.title: Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search
items.description: Search has been proposed as an effective method for self-improving language models and agentic systems, both for post-training sample generation and for inference. However, widely used methods such as best-of-N sampling and tree search face two fundamental limitations: they are guided by sparse verification signals, and they construct candidates primarily through autoregressive expansion, restricting exploration to regions with substantial model probability mass. items.summary: Search has been proposed as an effective method for self-improving language models and agentic systems, both for post-training sample generation and for inference. However, widely used methods such as best-of-N sampling and tree search face two fundamental limitations: they are guided by sparse verification signals, and they construct candidates primarily through autoregressive expansion, restricting exploration to regions with substantial model probability mass. To address these, we propose Bidirectional Evolutionary Search (BES), a search framework that couples forward candidate evolution with backward goal decomposition. In the forward search, BES augments standard expansion with evolution operators that recombine partial trajectories to generate candidates that are difficult to obtain from a single model rollout. In the backward search, BES recursively decomposes the original task into checkable subgoals, producing dense intermediate feedback that guides forward search. We provide theoretical motivation showing that candidates generated by expansion-only search are confined to a narrow entropy shell while evolutionary operators can escape it, and that backward search can exponentially reduce the number of required samples to find a correct answer. Experiments show that on challenging post-training tasks where mainstream post-training algorithms fail to improve, BES enables consistent gains, and on three open problem solving benchmarks at inference time, BES outperforms existing open-source frameworks in both average and best-case performance. Code and trained models are available at https://github.com/Embodied-Minds-Lab/BES.
items.author: Guowei Xu, Zhenting Qi, Huangyuan Su, Weirui Ye, Himabindu Lakkaraju, Sham M. Kakade, Yilun Du

normalizedKeys: title:area attribute extraction and aggregation for clip based class incremental learning

items.title: AREA: Attribute Extraction and Aggregation for CLIP-Based Class-Incremental Learning
items.description: Class-Incremental Learning (CIL) is important in building real-world learning systems. In CLIP-based CIL, the model performs classification by comparing similarity between visual and textual embeddings obtained from template prompts, e.g., ``a photo of a [CLASS]''. This seemingly monolithic matching process can be decomposed into two conceptually distinct stages: attribute extraction and attribute aggregation. For example, a model may recognize cat using attributes such as fur texture and whiske
items.summary: Class-Incremental Learning (CIL) is important in building real-world learning systems. In CLIP-based CIL, the model performs classification by comparing similarity between visual and textual embeddings obtained from template prompts, e.g., ``a photo of a [CLASS]''. This seemingly monolithic matching process can be decomposed into two conceptually distinct stages: attribute extraction and attribute aggregation. For example, a model may recognize cat using attributes such as fur texture and whiskers. When learning a new class like car, the model must extract additional attributes like wheels and adjust how they are aggregated in the shared representation space. However, since only data from the current task is available, incremental updates can bias both attribute extraction and aggregation toward new classes, leading to catastrophic forgetting. Therefore, we propose AREA for attribute extraction and aggregation in CLIP-based CIL. To stabilize extraction, we anchor class-level visual and textual attributes on the hyperspherical embedding space via principal geodesic analysis. To stabilize aggregation, we learn lightweight task-specific experts with scoring and residual refinement, regularized by a variational information bottleneck objective. During inference, we perform routing over task attribute manifolds via optimal transport for more concise prediction. Experiments show that AREA consistently outperforms SOTA methods. Code is available at https://github.com/LAMDA-CL/ICML2026-AREA.
items.author: Zhen-Hao Xie, Yu-Cheng Shi, Da-Wei Zhou
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: embedding
